policy_name: Organ, Tissue, and Eye Donation Policy
purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> supports organ, tissue, and eye donation: required testing on potential donors, specimen handling, result release. Required by 21 CFR 1271 (Human Cells, Tissues, and Cellular and Tissue-Based Products / HCT/Ps) and 42 CFR 493.1242 (specimen handling) and 42 CFR 493.1271 (immunohematology for transfusion in donor support).

cfr_text_blocks.label: FDA: HCT/P donor eligibility testing
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: You must test a donor specimen for evidence of infection due to relevant communicable disease agents, with the exception of CJD and other transmissible spongiform encephalopathies, listed in 1271.85(a)(1) using appropriate FDA-licensed, approved, or cleared donor screening tests, in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions.

scope: <<LAB_NAME>> personnel performing donor eligibility testing for organ procurement organizations, tissue banks, or eye banks.
policy_statements: Donor specimens for HCT/P eligibility are tested using FDA-licensed, approved, or cleared donor screening tests per 21 CFR 1271.85.
Required infectious-disease markers (HIV-1/2, HBV, HCV, HTLV-I/II, syphilis treponemal, others per current FDA list) are tested per the manufacturer's instructions.
Results are reported to the procuring organization with full specimen identification, test methods, reactive/non-reactive determination, and any limitations.
Records of donor testing are retained per 21 CFR 1271.270 (typically 10 years past distribution) and 42 CFR 493.1105.
procedure_steps: Specimens received from organ procurement, tissue bank, or eye bank are accessioned with full traceability to the procuring organization and donor ID.
Testing is performed using current FDA-cleared donor screening tests per the manufacturer's instructions.
Results are reported in writing to the procuring organization with all required elements.
Records are retained per the longer of 21 CFR 1271.270 and 42 CFR 493.1105.
definitions: Human Cells, Tissues, and Cellular and Tissue-Based Products. Regulated by FDA under 21 CFR 1271.
Infectious disease screening required for HCT/P donors to confirm absence of communicable disease agents per 21 CFR 1271.85.
